title: Seizure with Fever - Febrile Seizure
chief_complaint: 2-year-old with generalized tonic-clonic seizure lasting 3 minutes at home, now post-ictal. Temperature 39.8°C. No prior seizures. Normal development.

physical_exam.neuro: Post-ictal drowsiness, no focal deficits, no meningismus

correct_diagnosis: Simple Febrile Seizure (Secondary to Streptococcal Pharyngitis)
key_learning_points: No routine EEG or anticonvulsants needed

patient_responses.seizure: He shook all over for about 3 minutes, then got sleepy
patient_responses.development: He's been completely normal, walked at 12 months

hints.workup: Rule out meningitis and metabolic causes

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Simple febrile seizure - reassuring prognosis.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a simple febrile seizure. The age, fever, and normal neuro exam are key.
feedback.attending_note: Simple febrile seizure: Reassure parents - normal development, excellent prognosis, no increased epilepsy risk. Treat underlying infection. Antipyretics for comfort (do not prevent seizures). No anticonvulsants needed. Return precautions: seizure >5min or focal.
